info.title: Atlas API
info.description: Atlas knowledge and project-management platform REST API

post.summary: Completes account activation in a single DB transaction.
post.description:
Atomicity invariant: the row lock (`FOR UPDATE` on the token SELECT) combined
with the guarded consume (`AND consumed_at IS NULL` on the UPDATE plus a
rows-affected check) guarantee that exactly one activation succeeds per token
even under concurrent POST requests. Under READ COMMITTED, two racers that
reach the SELECT simultaneously will serialize at the lock: the loser blocks
until the winner commits, at which point the loser's `FOR UPDATE` re-read
sees the now-consumed row, the guarded UPDATE matches 0 rows, and the
transaction is rolled back with a 404 — no double-activation, no
double-session.

Steps inside the transaction:
1. Re-validate and lock the token row (`FOR UPDATE`).
2. Consume the token (`AND consumed_at IS NULL`); abort if already consumed.
3. Set the user's `password_hash` and `activated_at`.
4. Create a new session for the newly activated user.

Any failure rolls back the entire transaction: the user stays pending,
the token stays unconsumed, and no session is issued.

200.description: Platform security audit log (workspace_id IS NULL)

get.summary:
Returns every workspace in the system, ordered by creation date.
Restricted to root users via `RequireUserAdmin`.

delete.summary:
Soft-deletes a workspace, hiding it and its content from every lookup while
preserving the underlying rows. Restricted to root/system-admin via
`RequireUserAdmin`. Deleting an unknown or already-deleted workspace is a 404.

patch.summary: Updates a workspace's `name` and/or `slug` on behalf of a platform admin.
patch.description:
Unlike the member-facing `update_workspace`, this admin path may re-slug a
workspace. The new slug is format-validated and checked for collisions against
every existing slug (a soft-deleted workspace still reserves its slug). Both
fields are optional; omitting both is a no-op that returns the current state.
Restricted to root/system-admin via `RequireUserAdmin`.

422.description: Validation error or slug already in use

200.description: Paginated list of the caller's API keys

404.description: Key not found or already revoked

patch.summary:
Updates a user-owned API key. `is_global` and `scopes` are each PATCH-partial:
omitting a field leaves it unchanged; both may be set in the same request.
patch.description:
Any owner of the key may toggle global reach or replace its scope set; the
agent never gains more than its creator can reach (and stays capped at
editor) nor more capabilities than the closed catalog allows, so this is
bounded by the owner's own permissions rather than being a privilege
escalation.

400.description: Scopes present but empty; revoke the key instead

403.description: API keys cannot manage API keys

404.description: Key not found or not owned by the caller

200.description: Grants belonging to this API key

403.description: API keys cannot change a user password

get.summary: Returns the authenticated human user's stored UI state.
get.description:
The state is an opaque JSON object owned by the client. When the user has no
row yet, an empty object `{}` is returned. API keys (agents) are rejected
with 403: agents have no UI.

200.description: The caller's stored UI state, or an empty object

403.description: API keys have no UI state

put.summary: Inserts or replaces the authenticated human user's UI state.
put.description:
The `state` JSON object is stored verbatim; the server does not validate its
inner shape. The stored value is echoed back. API keys (agents) are rejected
with 403: agents have no UI.

200.description: The stored UI state, echoed back

403.description: API keys have no UI state

201.description: Pending user created with activation link

403.description: API keys cannot update a user profile
Not a root/admin user, or target is protected

200.description: Workspaces the user belongs to, with role

204.description: Password reset and all sessions revoked

403.description: Not a root/admin user, or target is protected

400.description: Cannot target self or a root user

get.summary: Returns the workspaces the authenticated principal can access.
get.description:
For users: their member workspaces (or all workspaces for root/system_admin).
For api_keys: a global key mirrors its creator's reach (so it does NOT need
per-workspace grants); a non-global key lists the workspaces where it holds at
least one grant.

post.summary: Creates a new workspace owned by the authenticated human user.
post.description:
The slug is derived from the name and de-duplicated against existing
workspace slugs. The creating user is added as `Owner`, so the workspace
immediately appears in `GET /api/workspaces`. API keys (agents) are rejected
with 403: agents are workspace-scoped and must not create workspaces.

404.description: Workspace not found or not a member

patch.summary:
Renames the workspace display name. The slug is never re-derived; only
`name` and `updated_at` change. Requires workspace membership.
patch.description:
A human member passes unchanged; an API-key principal is additionally
required to hold `config:update`, so a scoped agent cannot rename the
workspace without that capability. `member.api_key_id` is `Some` exactly for
those callers.

404.description: Workspace not found or not a member
Workspace not found or caller is not a member

get.summary: Lists users who can be added to the workspace.
get.description:
Returns the active (non-disabled) users that are NOT already members of this
workspace. The `users` table holds only human accounts, so api keys are
excluded by construction. Mirrors the `GET /api/users` shape (`Vec<UserDto>`).

200.description: Users that can be added as members

403.description: Caller is not workspace owner or admin

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Opaque cursor for the next page

404.description: Workspace not found or caller is not an admin
Workspace not found or caller is not an admin
Rule not found or caller is not an admin
Rule not found or caller is not an admin
Rule not found or caller is not an admin

422.description: Comment body is blank or exceeds the maximum length

403.description: Neither the comment's author nor a workspace admin/owner
Only the comment's author may edit it

422.description: Comment body is blank or exceeds the maximum length

404.description: Workspace not found or caller is not a member

403.description: Insufficient privileges — owner or admin required

409.description: A group with this name already exists in the workspace

422.description: User is not a workspace member

404.description: Workspace not found or caller is not an admin
Workspace not found or caller is not an admin
Config not found or caller is not an admin

204.description: Config soft-deleted and api key revoked

404.description: Config not found or caller is not an admin
Config not found or caller is not an admin

post.summary:
Receives a signed GitHub delivery, verifies it via the `VerifiedIntegrationEvent`
extractor, and hands it off to `AutomationService` for dedup, outbox storage,
and rule evaluation.
post.description:
Returns 200 regardless of whether the delivery was new or a duplicate so
that GitHub does not retry on idempotent re-deliveries.

404.description: No active integration config found for this workspace
Workspace not found or principal is not a member

post.summary: Adds an existing user to the workspace at the requested role.
post.description:
Mirrors the authorization discipline of `update_member_role`:
1. `WorkspaceOwnerOrAdmin` gates structural access (resolves caller class).
2. Role string is parsed; an unknown value → 422.
3. Role-grant matrix: an admin may add `member` or `admin`, never `owner`;
   only an owner (or break-glass) may add an `owner`.
4. The target user must exist (404) and must not be disabled (422 — a
   deactivated account cannot be assigned).
5. The user must not already be a member → 409 Conflict.
6. The membership insert and its audit row share one transaction.

403.description: Insufficient privileges to grant the requested role

404.description: Workspace or target user not found

422.description: Unknown role string, or target user is disabled

delete.summary: Removes a member from the workspace.
delete.description:
Enforces the same ordered authorization checks as `update_member_role`, adapted for
DELETE:
- Step 3: admin cannot remove an owner.
- Step 4: no one (including break-glass) can remove the last owner → 409.

404.description: Workspace or target member not found

patch.description:
Enforces strictly ordered authorization checks:
1. `WorkspaceOwnerOrAdmin` extractor gates structural access (resolves caller class).
2. Target membership is loaded; 404 if the target is not a member.
3. Role-permission matrix: admins cannot touch owners or promote to owner; others
   are allowed subject to step 4.
4. Last-owner-lockout (independent of step 3, applies to break-glass too): demoting
   the sole remaining owner → 409. This is a data-integrity invariant, not a
   permission — break-glass is NOT exempt.
5. Apply `update_role`. Idempotent: same-role PATCH validates the full matrix first.

Steps 3 and 4 are independent and strictly ordered. Never collapse them.

404.description: Workspace or target member not found

parameters.name: applies_to
parameters.description: Filter by applicability: task | document | both

409.description: A field with the derived key already exists

404.description: Property definition not found or not in workspace

200.description: Caller's saved searches sorted by name (case-insensitive)

404.description: Workspace not found or caller is not a member
Workspace not found or caller is not a member

409.description: A saved search with this name already exists for this owner

422.description: Validation error or per-owner cap exceeded

404.description: Saved search not found, not owned by caller, or caller is not a member
Saved search not found, not owned by caller, or caller is not a member

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search query (required). Supports key:value filter tokens.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Comma-separated kinds: note, task (e.g. note,task). 'all' or empty = no restriction (default). Unknown values are ignored.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor; must match the sort of the issuing request

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: When true, match each query word as a prefix (typeahead). Default false.

items.description: A single search result item returned by `GET /api/workspaces/{ws}/search`.

column_name.description: Current column (status) name. Present only when `kind = task`.

project_slug.description: Owning project slug; absent for workspace-root documents with no project.

readable_id.description: Task readable ID (e.g. `"ATL-42"`). Present only when `kind = task`.

snippet.description:
Highlighted snippet with `<mark>…</mark>` markers.
Absent for title-only matches and filter-only queries.

404.description: Workspace not found or principal has no access

422.description: Invalid input: absent q, malformed cursor, or cursor/sort mismatch

404.description: Workspace not found or principal has no access

422.description: Invalid input: absent q or malformed cursor

503.description: Semantic search is disabled or embedding provider is unavailable

200.description: Workspace status templates ordered by position
Distinct label strings used by non-deleted tasks

404.description: Tag not found or not in workspace
Tag not found or not in workspace

409.description: Name already taken by another tag

422.description: Invalid input (blank name or unknown color swatch)

200.description: Caller's task views sorted by name (case-insensitive)

404.description: Workspace not found or caller is not a member
Workspace not found or caller is not a member

409.description: A task view with this name already exists for this owner

422.description: Validation error or per-owner cap exceeded

404.description: Task view not found, not owned by caller, or caller is not a member
Task view not found, not owned by caller, or caller is not a member
Task view not found, not owned by caller, or caller is not a member

409.description: A task view with this name already exists for this owner

parameters.name: column_id
parameters.description: Filter by column id (repeat for multiple)

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort order: updated_at_desc (default), updated_at_asc, created_at_desc, created_at_asc, priority_desc, title_asc

404.description: Workspace not found or caller is not a member

post.summary: Uploads a file and attaches it to the resolved task.
post.description:
The body is `multipart/form-data` with the file in a part named `file`. Bytes are
streamed and accumulated up to `max_attachment_bytes`; the first chunk that would
exceed the cap aborts the upload with 413 so an oversize body is never fully
buffered. The stored blob is content-addressed, so re-uploading identical bytes
reuses the existing object.

requestBody.description: Multipart form-data carrying the file in a part named `file`

422.description: Missing file part or invalid file name

delete.description:
Only the DB row is marked deleted; the content-addressed blob is left in place
because the same bytes may be referenced by other attachments.

get.summary: Streams an attachment's bytes through the API for the resolved task.
get.description:
The attachment must belong to this task; an attachment owned by another task (or
a document) resolves to 404 rather than leaking across owners. Bytes are fetched
from the configured `AttachmentStore`, so this works for both the disk and S3
backends.

422.description: Comment body is blank or exceeds the maximum length

403.description: Neither the comment's author nor a workspace admin/owner
Only the comment's author may edit it

422.description: Comment body is blank or exceeds the maximum length

200.description: Sub-task promoted to a board task

post.summary: Creates a reference from the authorized source task to exactly one target.
post.description:
`task_references` targets are backed by foreign-key columns: the row cannot
be stored without a real target in this workspace. Both-null and both-set
bodies are rejected here as 422 before reaching the DB, preventing a CHECK
constraint violation or a silent both-null insert.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Opaque cursor for the next page

403.description: Caller is not a workspace admin or owner

404.description: Workspace not found or caller is not a member

201.description: Subscription created; secret is returned here and never again

403.description: Caller is not a workspace admin or owner

404.description: Workspace not found or caller is not a member

422.description: Validation error (invalid event types, scope, or URL)

403.description: Caller is not a workspace admin or owner
Caller is not a workspace admin or owner
Caller is not a workspace admin or owner
Caller is not a workspace admin or owner

get.summary: Readiness probe: liveness plus a `SELECT 1` round-trip to the database.
get.description:
Unlike `/health` (a cheap liveness signal), this endpoint touches the pool so
an orchestrator can withhold traffic while the database is unreachable or the
pool is exhausted. Returns 503 on any database error.

200.description: Service is ready: the database is reachable

503.description: Service is not ready: the database is unreachable

ActivationInfoDto.description:
Minimal user info returned by `GET /api/activate/{token}` so the activation
page can display a personalised heading without requiring authentication.

ActivationLinkResponse.description:
Response from `POST /api/users/{user_id}/activation-link`.

`activation_link` is a freshly issued single-use link. Prior unconsumed tokens
for the same user are invalidated.

activation_link.description: Single-use activation path (e.g. `/activate/<token>`). Share this with the invitee.

ActivityEntryDto.description: A single activity entry on a task.

task_id.description: The task this activity entry belongs to.

ActorDto.description: Actor attribution attached to revisions and attachments.

account_status.description:
Account lifecycle state for user assignees: `"active"`, `"deactivated"`, or `"pending"`.
Absent for api_key actors and for all attribution contexts (created_by, assigned_by, etc.).

AddMemberRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/workspaces/{ws}/members`.

Adds an existing user to the workspace at the given role. `role` is one of
`"owner"` | `"admin"` | `"member"`; granting `owner` requires an owner (or
break-glass) caller.

AdminUpdateWorkspaceRequest.description:
Request body for `PATCH /api/admin/workspaces/{ws}` (root/system-admin only).

Both fields are optional and applied independently: omit a field to leave it
unchanged. Unlike the member-facing `UpdateWorkspaceRequest`, this admin path
also allows re-slugging a workspace.

AgentIdentityDto.description:
The self-identity of an API-key (agent) principal, returned in the `agent`
field of `GET /api/auth/me` so an agent can inspect its own id, name, and the
capability scopes it currently holds.

scopes.description: The key's capability scopes, in canonical `family:action` order.
The key's capability scopes, in canonical `family:action` order.

secret.description: The full `atlas_`-prefixed secret. Shown exactly once; not stored.

ApiKeyDto.description: Summary representation of an API key (no secret).

is_global.description:
When true, the key reaches every workspace its creator can reach (capped at
editor), instead of only workspaces where it holds an explicit grant.

scopes.description: The key's capability scopes, in canonical `family:action` order.

ApiKeyGrantDto.description:
A single grant belonging to an API key, with resolved resource labels.

Returned by `GET /api/api-keys/{key_id}/grants` so the keys panel can display
human-readable resource names without additional lookups.

oneOf.description: Who created this grant. `None` for legacy/system grants with no recorded creator.

resource_label.description: Human-readable label: workspace/project name, or id+kind for sub-resources.

workspace_slug.description: Workspace slug (always present — all grants live inside a workspace).

ApiKeyScope.description:
The closed catalog of API key capability scopes: resource families crossed
with the four CRUD actions (`read`, `create`, `update`, `delete`), except
`grants`, which is read-only and contributes only `grants:read`.

This is the wire mirror of `atlas_domain::permissions::Capability`; the
server maps between the two at the route boundary. Being a closed serde
enum, an unrecognized wire value (e.g. `"tasks:manage"`) is rejected during
deserialization with a 422, before any handler runs.

AssigneeDto.description: An actor assigned to a task.

AuditEntryDto.description: A single security audit log entry.

target_label.description:
Human-readable label for the target (e.g. user display_name or api key name),
resolved cheaply when target_type is "user" or "api_key".

BacklinkDto.description: A document that links to this document (backlink).

BoardPresenceResponse.description:
The set of principals currently present on a board.

Returned by the presence heartbeat endpoint so a client learns the full
visible set on its first heartbeat, and mirrored inside the `data` field of a
`presence.updated` live event so both transports share one shape.

BoardSummaryDto.description: Lightweight board summary for list endpoints.

promoted_task_id.description: Set once the item has been promoted to a task.

ColumnDto.description: Column representation (always returned in board context).

CommentDto.description:
A single markdown comment on a task or a document.

Comments are polymorphic: exactly one of `task_id` / `document_id` is present,
identifying the owner the comment was loaded from.

ConflictProblemDto.description:
RFC 9457 problem+json extension for CAS revision conflicts (status 409).

Flattens `ProblemDetails` fields alongside conflict-specific fields so the
client receives a single `application/problem+json` body.

base_to_current_patch.description: Unified diff from the client's base revision to the current head.

current_revision_id.description: The revision ID that is currently head (the client's token is stale).

current_seq.description: Sequence number of the current head revision.

CopyDocumentRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/workspaces/{ws}/documents/{slug}/copy`.

`folder_id` is the destination folder for the copy. When omitted, the copy
lands in the same folder as the source document.

CopyFolderRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/workspaces/{ws}/folders/{folder_id}/copy`.

`parent_folder_id` is the destination parent for the copied top folder. When
omitted, the copy lands under the same parent as the source folder.

action_params.description: Parameters for the action. For `"create_task"`: `{board_id, column_id, title_template, priority?}`.

action_type.description: Action to execute on match. Only `"create_task"` is supported in v1.

project_id.description: Reserved for future project-scoped automation; must be omitted in v1.

trigger_filter.description: Optional top-level string-equality filter. All keys must match for the rule to fire.

CreateDocumentRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/workspaces/{ws}/projects/{ps}/documents`.

The slug is always server-generated; any slug field provided by the client
is silently ignored.

integration.description: Integration slug. Only `"github"` is supported in v1.

CreatePropertyDefinitionRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/workspaces/{ws}/property-definitions`.

The `key` is derived server-side from `name`; callers do not supply it.

applies_to.description: One of `document` | `task` | `both`. Defaults to `task` when omitted.

options.description:
Required for `select`/`multi_select` (a non-empty array of unique
non-empty strings); must be absent/null for the other kinds.

CreateUserApiKeyRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/api-keys` (top-level, user-owned key creation).

oneOf.description: Optional initial grant so the key is immediately usable in one workspace.

scopes.description:
Capability scopes to grant the new key. Omitted or empty defaults to
read-only access to every family (`{family}:read` for all five families).

CreateUserRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/users`.

Creates a pending account with no password. The returned `activation_link`
must be shared with the invitee so they can set their own password.

role.description: Membership role: `"admin"` or `"member"` (owner is rejected with 422).

workspace.description: Workspace slug where the new user will be added.

CreateUserResponse.description:
Response from `POST /api/users`.

`activation_link` is the plaintext single-use link shown exactly once.
It is not stored — only the hash is kept server-side.

activation_link.description: Single-use activation path (e.g. `/activate/<token>`). Share this with the invitee.

event_types.description: At least one event-type string is required.

label.description: Optional human-readable label for the subscription.

target_url.description: Absolute HTTPS (or HTTP for local testing) URL to POST events to.

DocumentPresenceResponse.description:
The set of principals currently present on a document.

Returned by the document presence heartbeat endpoint so a client learns the
full visible set — and the document's canonical UUID — on its first heartbeat,
and mirrored inside the `data` field of a `presence.updated` live event so both
transports share one shape. `document_id` lets a client that addressed the
document by slug correlate subsequent broadcasts, which are keyed by UUID.

DocumentSummaryDto.description: Lightweight document summary for list endpoints.

FolderDto.description: Folder representation returned by all folder endpoints.

FrontmatterDto.description: Document frontmatter extracted from the leading YAML block.

GrantedByDto.description:
The principal that created a grant, resolved to a human-readable label.

Distinguishes a human granter (`principal_type` `"user"`, `display` is the
user's display name) from an agent granter (`principal_type` `"api_key"`,
`display` is the key's name) so the UI can render the two unambiguously.

InitialGrantRequest.description:
Optional initial workspace grant included in a `POST /api/api-keys` request.

When present, a workspace-scope grant at the given role is created atomically
with the key so the key is immediately usable in that workspace.

role.description: Role: `"viewer"` or `"editor"` (admin is rejected by the agent cap).

workspace.description: Workspace slug for the initial grant.

IntegrationConfigCreatedDto.description:
Response from `POST /api/workspaces/{ws}/integration-configs`.

Carries the HMAC secret in plaintext exactly once. After this response the
secret is never available again — configure your integration immediately.

secret.description: Plaintext HMAC-SHA256 signing secret (`integ_…`). Shown exactly once.

IntegrationConfigDto.description:
Integration config as returned by list and get.

Never contains the plaintext or encrypted HMAC secret.

integration_api_key_id.description: Provisioned `ApiKeyType::Integration` key used as the automation actor.

token.description: Opaque session token — also delivered as an HttpOnly cookie.

oneOf.description:
Self-identity for an API-key principal: its id, name, and capability
scopes. `None` for human user and group principals.

parent_folder_id.description: New parent folder ID, or `null` to move to the project root.

items.description: A single activity entry on a task.

task_id.description: The task this activity entry belongs to.

items.description: Summary representation of an API key (no secret).

is_global.description:
When true, the key reaches every workspace its creator can reach (capped at
editor), instead of only workspaces where it holds an explicit grant.

scopes.description: The key's capability scopes, in canonical `family:action` order.

items.description: A single security audit log entry.

target_label.description:
Human-readable label for the target (e.g. user display_name or api key name),
resolved cheaply when target_type is "user" or "api_key".

items.description: A document that links to this document (backlink).
Lightweight board summary for list endpoints.

A single markdown comment on a task or a document.

Comments are polymorphic: exactly one of `task_id` / `document_id` is present,
identifying the owner the comment was loaded from.
Lightweight document summary for list endpoints.
Folder representation returned by all folder endpoints.
An inbound reference — another task that points to this one.
Lightweight task summary for list endpoints.

assignees.description:
Assigned actors (users and agents), resolved with display names so the
kanban card can render an avatar and the agent badge without a follow-up
request.

board_name.description: Name of the board this task belongs to.

column_name.description: Name of the column (status) this task is currently in.

estimate.description:
Non-negative work estimate in story-point units; surfaced inline (e.g. on a
sub-task row) without loading the full task.

subtask_count.description:
Number of direct sub-tasks (children whose `parent_task_id` is this task),
so a list view can flag tasks that have sub-tasks without a follow-up
request. Zero for tasks with no children.

items.description: One delivery-attempt log entry for a subscription.

Subscription representation returned by list, get, and update.

Never contains the HMAC secret or any ciphertext. The plaintext secret is
returned exactly once in `WebhookCreatedDto` at creation time.

event_types.description: Ordered list of event-type strings this subscription receives.

scope_id.description: UUID of the project or board when `scope_type` is not `"workspace"`.

PatchAutomationRuleRequest.description:
Partial update for `PATCH /api/workspaces/{ws}/automation-rules/{rule_id}`.

Omitted fields are left unchanged. Pass `{"trigger_filter": null}` to clear the filter.

trigger_filter.description: `Some(null)` clears the filter; `Some(value)` replaces it; absent leaves it unchanged.

PrincipalDto.description:
A principal (workspace member or agent) that a grant can be addressed to.

Returned by `GET /api/workspaces/{ws}/members` so the share dialog can resolve
a human-readable name to the principal id required by a grant request.

account_status.description:
Account lifecycle state for user principals: `"active"`, `"deactivated"`, or `"pending"`.
Absent for `api_key` principals.

display.description: Display name: the user's display name, or the api key's name.

principal_type.description: `"user"` for a workspace member, `"api_key"` for an agent.

role.description:
Workspace membership role (`"owner"` | `"admin"` | `"member"`).
Present for `user` principals; absent for `api_key` principals.

ProblemDetails.description: RFC 9457 problem+json representation, extended with `request_id` and `hint`.

hint.description: Actionable next-step hint for the caller.

instance.description: Request path — filled by the problem-stamp middleware.

request_id.description: Filled by the problem-stamp middleware from the `x-request-id` header.

title.description: Human-readable summary of the error class.

type.description: Stable error type URI in the form `urn:atlas:error:{slug}`.

board_id.description: Target board for the new task.

column_id.description: Target column for the new task.

key.description: Stable machine key derived from the name (`^[a-z][a-z0-9_]{0,63}$`).

options.description:
For `select`/`multi_select`: the allowed string options. Absent for the
other kinds.

ReferenceDto.description: A typed outbound reference from a task to another task or document.

target_resolved.description: False when the target no longer exists (broken ref), consistent with E04.

target_title.description:
Title of the target document, for display. Present only for resolved
document references (task references use `target_readable_id`).

ReferenceOriginDto.description: The source of a task-reference entry in the unified read model.

RevisionContentDto.description: Full revision content at a specific sequence number.

RevisionMetaDto.description: Revision metadata returned by the history endpoint.

SavedSearchDto.description: Saved search representation. Does not expose the owner principal.

SearchHitDto.description: A single search result item returned by `GET /api/workspaces/{ws}/search`.

column_name.description: Current column (status) name. Present only when `kind = task`.

project_slug.description: Owning project slug; absent for workspace-root documents with no project.

readable_id.description: Task readable ID (e.g. `"ATL-42"`). Present only when `kind = task`.

snippet.description:
Highlighted snippet with `<mark>…</mark>` markers.
Absent for title-only matches and filter-only queries.

SearchKindDto.description: Discriminant indicating whether a search hit is a document or a task.

ServerMetaDto.description: Response from `GET /api/meta`. Server build information for the About screen.

url.description: Public base URL of this server, when configured (`ATLAS_SERVER_URL`).

TaskAttachmentDto.description:
Metadata for a file attached to a task.

The binary content is not inlined; it is fetched separately from the task's
attachment content endpoint, which streams the bytes through the API.

TaskBacklinkDto.description: An inbound reference — another task that points to this one.

board_name.description: Name of the board this task belongs to.

column_name.description: Name of the column (status) this task is currently in.

parent_task_id.description: Set when this task is a sub-task of another; absent for top-level tasks.

TaskPropertiesDto.description: Typed task properties validated at the API boundary.

custom.description: Free-form JSONB escape hatch; no workspace-schema validation in M1.

estimate.description: Non-negative work estimate in story-point units.

TaskSummaryDto.description: Lightweight task summary for list endpoints.

assignees.description:
Assigned actors (users and agents), resolved with display names so the
kanban card can render an avatar and the agent badge without a follow-up
request.

board_name.description: Name of the board this task belongs to.

column_name.description: Name of the column (status) this task is currently in.

estimate.description:
Non-negative work estimate in story-point units; surfaced inline (e.g. on a
sub-task row) without loading the full task.

subtask_count.description:
Number of direct sub-tasks (children whose `parent_task_id` is this task),
so a list view can flag tasks that have sub-tasks without a follow-up
request. Zero for tasks with no children.

TaskViewDto.description: Task view representation. Does not expose the owner principal.

TaskViewFiltersDto.description:
Task view filter set as exposed at the API boundary.

All fields are optional. An empty `{}` is a valid "all workspace tasks" view.

assignee.description: Assignee filter. Encoded as a string: "me", "user:{uuid}", "api_key:{uuid}".

column_ids.description: Restrict to these board column ids.

labels.description: Restrict to tasks carrying ALL of these labels.

priorities.description: Restrict to these priority levels. Valid values: low, medium, high, urgent.

sort.description:
Sort order. Valid values: updated_at_desc, updated_at_asc, created_at_desc,
created_at_asc, priority_desc, title_asc.

UiStateDto.description:
Response from `GET /api/me/ui-state` and `PUT /api/me/ui-state`.

`state` is an opaque JSON object owned by the client (e.g. which sidebar
folders are collapsed). The server stores and returns it verbatim and does
not validate its inner shape.

UnifiedReferenceDto.description: A unified outbound reference read from manual references and task-description wikilinks.

UpdateApiKeyRequest.description:
Request body for `PATCH /api/api-keys/{key_id}`.

Both fields are PATCH-partial: omit a field to leave it unchanged.

is_global.description: Toggles the key's global reach. Omit to leave unchanged.

scopes.description:
Replaces the key's full scope set. Omit to leave unchanged; an explicit
empty list is rejected (revoke the key instead of deny-all scopes).

UpdateColumnRequest.description:
Request body for `PATCH /api/workspaces/{ws}/boards/{board_id}/columns/{column_id}`.

Color uses the same `present_value` convention as `UpdateTaskRequest` fields:
an absent `color` key leaves the color unchanged; explicit `null` clears it;
a string value sets it. `name`, `before`, and `after` remain simple `Option<String>`.

UpdateContentRequest.description:
Request body for `PUT /api/workspaces/{ws}/documents/{slug}/content`.

Uses compare-and-swap semantics: `base_revision_id` must match the
document's current revision or the server responds with 409 and a
`ConflictProblemDto` body.

UpdateDocumentRequest.description:
Request body for `PATCH /api/workspaces/{ws}/documents/{slug}`.

Updates document metadata (title, folder). Use `PUT .../content` to update
content with CAS.

UpdateIntegrationConfigRequest.description:
Request body for `PATCH /api/workspaces/{ws}/integration-configs/{config_id}`.

Only `is_active` can be changed; the integration slug and secret are fixed at
creation. Omitting `is_active` leaves the config unchanged. Setting it to
`false` makes the inbound ingest reject events for this integration.

UpdateMeRequest.description: Request body for `PATCH /api/users/me`. Only the provided fields are updated.

UpdateStatusTemplateRequest.description:
Request body for `PATCH /api/workspaces/{ws}/status-templates/{id}`.

`name`: `None` = leave unchanged; `Some(v)` = rename.
`color`: absent = leave unchanged; explicit `null` = clear; string = set.
`before`/`after`: position anchors for reorder.

UpdateTagRequest.description:
Request body for `PATCH /api/workspaces/{ws}/tags/{tag_id}`.

Both fields are optional: supply `name` to rename, `color` to recolor, or both.
`color: None` in the JSON payload is treated as "leave unchanged"; there is no
way to explicitly clear a tag color via this endpoint (NULL = unset).

UpdateUiStateRequest.description: Request body for `PUT /api/me/ui-state`. The `state` is stored verbatim.

UpdateWebhookRequest.description:
Request body for `PATCH /api/workspaces/{ws}/webhooks/{webhook_id}`.

All fields are optional; omitted fields are left unchanged.
The secret is never changed via this endpoint (no rotation endpoint in v1).

activated_at.description: `None` means the account has not yet been activated.

UserMembershipDto.description:
One workspace a user belongs to, with the membership role.

Returned by `GET /api/users/{user_id}/memberships` to power the admin
workspace-access editor, which assigns a user across workspaces.

WebhookCreatedDto.description:
Response from `POST /api/workspaces/{ws}/webhooks`.

Carries the HMAC secret in plaintext exactly once. After this response the
secret is never available again — store it immediately.

secret.description: Plaintext HMAC-SHA256 signing secret (`whsec_…`). Shown exactly once.

WebhookDeliveryDto.description: One delivery-attempt log entry for a subscription.

WebhookDto.description:
Subscription representation returned by list, get, and update.

Never contains the HMAC secret or any ciphertext. The plaintext secret is
returned exactly once in `WebhookCreatedDto` at creation time.

event_types.description: Ordered list of event-type strings this subscription receives.

scope_id.description: UUID of the project or board when `scope_type` is not `"workspace"`.

tags.name: integrations
tags.description: External integration config management and event ingestion (admin-only)
